skill.name: join-meeting
skill.description: AgentCall (agentcall.dev) — Join a video meeting (Google Meet, Teams, Zoom) as an AI bot with voice and visual presence. Supports audio-only mode with voice intelligence (barge-in, interruptions), text-to-speech mode, and webpage modes for custom UI. Use when asked to join a call, attend a meeting, or participate in a video conference.
skill.summary: Join video meetings as an AI agent with voice, avatar, screenshare, and real-time transcription.
skill.author: pattern-ai-labs

security_audit.summary: Community skill for joining video meetings via AgentCall (agentcall.dev). The vast majority of the 2690 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner triggering on Markdown backticks (1567 'external_commands'), crypto terms in API documentation ('weak crypto'), and example file code. After evaluation, the skill's genuine risk factors are: (1) network access to api.agentcall.dev and meeting platforms, (2) filesystem access for config (~/.agentcall/config.json) and state files, (3) env var access for AGENTCALL_API_KEY, and (4) subprocess invocation for the bridge/join scripts. These are expected and necessary for a meeting bot. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Network access to external API and meeting platforms
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTPS calls to api.agentcall.dev and to Google Meet, Zoom, and Teams URLs. This is required functionality for a meeting bot. No data exfiltration to unexpected endpoints was found.

medium_findings.title: API key stored in user config file
medium_findings.description: The skill stores the AgentCall API key in ~/.agentcall/config.json with file permissions. Key is read from AGENTCALL_API_KEY env var or the config file. This is expected for a paid API service. Users should protect the config file with appropriate permissions.

medium_findings.title: Subprocess execution for bridge and tunnel scripts
medium_findings.description: Scripts invoke subprocesses for meeting bridge and tunnel functionality. Arguments are hardcoded or user-supplied meeting URLs. No command injection vectors were found.

low_findings.title: Static scanner false positives: Markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The scanner reported 1567 'external_commands' findings, almost all of which are Markdown backticks in documentation files (SKILL.md, references/*.md, examples/*/README.md) used for code formatting, not actual shell execution. These are false positives.

low_findings.title: Static scanner false positives: 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' in API docs
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'weak crypto' in references/api.md and other docs, but these are descriptions of API endpoints, not actual cryptographic operations. The skill does not implement custom cryptography.

low_findings.title: Path traversal strings in documentation
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged '../' sequences in documentation describing URL path components for API endpoints. No actual path traversal vulnerability exists.

content.user_title: Join Meetings as an AI Agent
content.value_statement: Connecting AI agents to live video meetings is hard. This skill lets Claude or Codex join Google Meet, Zoom, or Teams calls as a voice-enabled bot with transcription, avatar, and screenshare support.
actual_capabilities: Join Google Meet, Zoom, and Microsoft Teams meetings as an AI bot
Speak and listen using text-to-speech and voice intelligence with barge-in support
Transcribe meeting audio in real time and stream events to the agent
Display an avatar or custom webpage during video calls
Share screens from the bot to meeting participants
Handle meeting interruptions, crashes, and clean exit with state recovery
limitations: Requires an AgentCall API key (free trial credits available for new accounts)
Audio quality and latency depend on network conditions and platform
Some meeting platforms may block bot participants by policy
Collaborative mode trigger words must be configured per meeting

use_cases.title: AI standup participant
use_cases.description: Deploy an AI agent to attend daily standups, report status, and answer questions when the team lead is unavailable.

use_cases.title: Customer interview notetaker
use_cases.description: Have an AI bot join customer calls, transcribe the conversation, and produce structured summaries and action items.

use_cases.title: Sales call assistant
use_cases.description: Join sales calls as a real-time assistant that provides talking points, answers product questions, and logs follow-ups.

prompt_templates.title: Join a meeting with default settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Join the meeting at https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij as AgentBot. Listen actively and take notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple meeting join with audio-only mode

prompt_templates.title: Join with avatar and screenshare
prompt_templates.prompt: Join https://zoom.us/j/123456789 using the pattern avatar and share my local dashboard on port 3000.

prompt_templates.title: Collaborative mode with trigger words
prompt_templates.prompt: Join the team call in collaborative mode. Only respond when you hear 'hey bot' or 'agent help'. Stay silent otherwise.

prompt_templates.title: Notetaker with structured output
prompt_templates.prompt: Join the customer interview and produce a JSON summary with key topics, decisions, and action items at the end.

output_examples.input: Join https://meet.google.com/xyz-abcd-efg and introduce yourself as the project assistant.
output_examples.output: Bot joins the meeting, announces 'Hello, I am the project assistant AI. How can I help?' and begins transcribing.

output_examples.input: Listen to the sales call and identify three key objections raised by the prospect.
output_examples.output: After the call, the agent reports: 1) pricing concerns, 2) integration complexity, 3) timeline mismatch.

output_examples.input: Join the standup and report blockers for the engineering team.
output_examples.output: Bot transcribes each update and outputs a summary: 'Blockers: CI pipeline delays, waiting on API access from vendor.'

best_practices: Store the AgentCall API key in ~/.agentcall/config.json with restricted file permissions rather than passing it in commands
Use collaborative mode for meetings where the bot should not speak unless addressed
Configure trigger words specific to each meeting context to avoid false activations
Test with the webpage-av-screenshare mode first to verify the bot appears correctly before important calls
anti_patterns: Do not share the API key in chat logs, screenshots, or public repositories
Do not use audio-only mode for meetings where visual context or screen sharing is critical
Do not leave the bot in meetings indefinitely; configure timeouts and exit conditions
Do not rely on the bot to handle sensitive negotiations without human oversight

faq.question: Which meeting platforms are supported?
faq.answer: Google Meet, Zoom, and Microsoft Teams are supported. The bot joins via standard meeting URLs.

faq.question: Do I need an AgentCall account?
faq.answer: Yes. Register at agentcall.dev using Google OAuth, generate an API key, and add credits. New accounts receive free trial credits.

faq.question: Can the bot speak in multiple languages?
faq.answer: Yes. The text-to-speech engine supports multiple languages. Configure the language in the join command or the agent context.

faq.question: What happens if the bot disconnects mid-meeting?
faq.answer: The skill includes crash recovery. Running the join command again will detect the existing call and rejoin without creating a duplicate.

faq.question: Is the meeting audio stored?
faq.answer: Audio is streamed to AgentCall servers for transcription and TTS. Check the AgentCall privacy policy for data retention details.

faq.question: Can I customize the bot's appearance?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes several avatar templates (pattern, orb, ring, avatar) and supports custom HTML webpages for the bot's video display.
